title: Ototoxicity, vincristine neuropathy, neurocognitive/psychosocial entries
context: A core HIGH-burden Ewing late-effect entry for ewing-survivorship-late-effects: Ototoxicity (platinum), vincristine neuropathy, and neurocognitive/psychosocial late effects. This is HIGH risk-tier patient-facing health information, so it is education-only with the 'Informational, not medical advice - consult your care team' label, every assertion sourced, the fact-vs-expression copyright firewall (synthesize facts and cite; never reproduce protected guideline text/tables/figures) upheld, and out-of-scope content (diagnosis, prognosis, dosing, individual risk score, trial-matching) excluded. Publish requires that no patient-facing page ships without BOTH a credentialed pediatric/AYA oncologist AND a patient-advocate version-scoped sign-off (neither alone suffices); until both are secured, content is drafted/held, not published.
objective: Draft the ototoxicity, vincristine-neuropathy, and neurocognitive/psychosocial sub-entries, each fully sourced, exposure-keyed, and dual-signed.
acceptanceCriteria: Each sub-entry fully sourced and exposure-keyed; no-advice label; seek-care routing for urgent symptoms; grade 6-8; WCAG 2.2 AA
Psychosocial/AYA mental-health framing reviewed by the advocate for harm-avoidance (no fear/fatalism)
Dual-signed (oncologist + advocate) and passes all automated gates
